examples: I want to improve my English.
Regular practice can improve your pronunciation.
The service has improved since last year.

definition: a choice made after thinking about different options
examples: She made an important decision.
It was a difficult decision to leave.
We need more information before making a decision.

definition: ready to be used or obtained
examples: Is this product available today?
The manager is not available now.
Several options are available online.

sentence: You can _____ your goals with practice.
examples: You can achieve your goals with practice.
She worked hard to achieve better results.
The team achieved its target.

definition: to need something for a particular purpose
examples: This task will require more time.
The job requires strong communication skills.
Some repairs require professional help.

definition: to become or make something larger in amount
examples: We need to increase production.
Prices increased again this month.
Exercise can increase your energy.

definition: to make something smaller or less
examples: The company wants to reduce costs.
We should reduce plastic use.
This medicine may reduce the pain.

definition: to help or encourage someone or something
examples: Good friends support each other.
The evidence supports his argument.
Her family supported her decision.

definition: to examine how two or more things are similar or different
examples: Let us compare the two options.
It is difficult to compare the products.
She compared prices before buying.

definition: to give someone something they need
examples: The course will provide useful information.
The hotel provides free breakfast.
Please provide your contact details.

definition: to stay away from something or prevent it from happening
examples: Try to avoid unnecessary mistakes.
He avoids driving during rush hour.
Regular checks help avoid problems.

definition: to grow, improve, or create something over time
examples: Reading helps you develop new skills.
The company is developing a new product.
Children develop at different speeds.

definition: to take something for a short time and return it later
sentence: Can I _____ your pen for a minute?
examples: Can I borrow your pen for a minute?
She borrowed a book from the library.
I borrowed some money from my brother.

definition: to decide which person or thing you want
sentence: You need to _____ one of these options.
examples: You need to choose one of these options.
She chose the blue dress.
It is hard to choose between two good offers.

definition: to reach a place at the end of a journey
sentence: We will _____ at the station at noon.
examples: We will arrive at the station at noon.
The package arrived this morning.
Please call me when you arrive.

sentence: Did you _____ the change in the room?
examples: Did you notice the change in the room?
I noticed a mistake in the report.
She did not notice the open door.

definition: to ask someone to come to an event or place
sentence: I want to _____ my friends to dinner.
examples: I want to invite my friends to dinner.
They invited us to their wedding.
Can I invite a colleague to the meeting?
